items.name: root
items.type: HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>; aria-label defaults to Table of contents
items.evidence: HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>; aria-label defaults to Table of contents

states.name: active
states.initial: exactly as supplied per Item or linked Group; no internal selection state

aria: root is a navigation landmark with default aria-label Table of contents
active Item emits aria-current=true; inactive Item omits aria-current
Group emits aria-current=true only when href is present and active is true
component is controlled/presentational: consumer owns scroll tracking and active state
Group always nests its children in a ul; label is an anchor when href exists and otherwise a paragraph
keys: native anchor navigation only; no component-managed keys

styles: block w-full truncate border-l-2 border-transparent py-0.5 pl-4 text-sm text-left no-underline
tokens: block w-full truncate border-l-2 border-transparent py-0.5 pl-4 text-sm text-left no-underline

unknowns.reason: Item render clones a consumer element with anchor-like props; its resulting tag, router behavior, and focus semantics depend on that supplied element and are not invented.
Canonical TableOfContents performs no observation or scroll tracking; which item is active and whether multiple active flags are supplied is wholly consumer-controlled.

canonical render operation is not yet represented by the implementation algebra
canonical state operation is not yet represented by the implementation algebra
